rationale: Both require the cloud PII processor to cooperate with and assist the customer (PII controller) in fulfilling obligations toward PII principals (data subjects).
Both prohibit the cloud/PII processor from processing PII for any purpose beyond the documented instructions of the customer/controller, limiting use to the agreed service purposes only.
Both explicitly prohibit use of PII for marketing or advertising without the consent of the PII principal, directly paralleling the ban on secondary commercial use by the processor.
ISO 27018 A.3.1 limits processor purpose to customer instructions; 27701-8.2.6 additionally requires maintaining records of all processing activities, going beyond the purpose-limitation statement.
Both require secure deletion/erasure of temporary files containing PII that arise during cloud/processor operations and are no longer needed.
Both require the processor to notify the customer/controller whenever it receives a legally binding request to disclose PII (e.g. from law enforcement), unless legally prohibited.

27018 A.6.1 covers notification of any disclosure request; 27701-8.5.6 specifically covers legally binding mandatory disclosures and requires handling them while informing the controller — overlapping but 27701 is more prescriptive on the handling procedure.
Both require the processor to maintain records of all PII disclosures to third parties, enabling accountability and auditability of every disclosure event.
Both require disclosure to the customer/controller of any subcontractors (sub-processors) used to process PII, enabling the controller to assess the sub-processing chain.

27018 A.8.1 requires disclosure of subcontractors; 27701-8.5.8 additionally requires the processor to notify the controller in advance of any changes to subcontractors, giving the controller an opportunity to object.

27018 A.10.1 requires notification of the customer in the event of a data breach involving PII; 27701-6.13.1 provides broader PII incident management guidance applicable to all organizations, but 27018 is cloud-processor-specific and more operationally prescriptive.

Breach notification to the controller supports the customer obligations in 27701-8.2.5 (assisting the controller), but 27018 A.10.1 is a specific operational trigger while 27701-8.2.5 is a general assistance obligation.

27018 A.10.2 requires retention of administrative security policies for a defined period; 27701-6.15.1 covers compliance with legal/contractual requirements including documentation retention, but is broader and not cloud-specific.
Both require the processor to have procedures for returning, transferring, or securely disposing of PII when the service agreement ends or at the controller's request.

27018 A.11.1 requires confidentiality or non-disclosure agreements with personnel processing PII; 27701-6.15.1 addresses contractual compliance more broadly, but both enforce binding confidentiality obligations.
27018 A.11.1 (confidentiality NDAs) relates to 27701-6.4.2 (during employment — personnel security), which requires awareness and binding obligations for employees handling PII.

27018 A.11.2 restricts creation of hardcopy material containing PII in cloud environments; 27701-6.9.1 addresses operational procedures and responsibilities including output controls, but 27701 does not have an equally specific hardcopy restriction.

27018 A.11.3 requires control and logging of data restoration operations to prevent unauthorized access to PII during restoration; 27701-6.9.4 (logging and monitoring) covers logging broadly, but 27018 is specifically focused on restoration events.
Both require protecting PII on storage media that leaves the premises, including encryption and secure handling to prevent unauthorized disclosure during transit.

27018 A.11.5 specifically addresses unencrypted portable storage media and requires encryption of PII; 27701-6.5.3 (media handling) covers the broader category of media protection, of which encryption of portable media is a specific sub-requirement.

The encryption requirement in 27018 A.11.5 for portable media aligns with 27701-6.7.1 (cryptographic controls), which provides the PIMS-extended guidance on when and how to apply cryptography to protect PII.
Both require encryption of PII transmitted over public networks, protecting PII in transit from unauthorized interception during cloud processing operations.

27018 A.11.6 mandates encryption for PII in public-network transit (cloud-specific); 27701-6.7.1 gives broader cryptographic controls guidance applicable to all PII transmission contexts, making it a superset in scope.

27018 A.11.7 requires secure disposal of hardcopy materials containing PII; 27701-6.5.3 (media handling) covers disposal of media broadly, but hardcopy-specific secure disposal is a sub-point within this broader control.

27018 A.11.8 mandates unique user IDs for all users accessing PII in cloud systems; 27701-6.6.2 (user access management) requires proper identity management including unique identifiers, of which this is a specific enforcement sub-point.

27018 A.11.9 requires maintaining records of authorized users who can access PII; 27701-6.6.2 requires user access management records, with 27018 adding the cloud-PII-specific documentation requirement.
Both require lifecycle management of user IDs including timely revocation/deactivation when users no longer require access to PII systems.

Both require that the processing relationship be governed by a formal contract specifying the nature, scope, and purpose of PII processing, establishing the customer agreement as the legal basis for processor activity.

27018 A.11.11 addresses contract measures for the processor side; 27701-7.2.6 addresses the same contractual requirement from the controller perspective, requiring controllers to have written contracts with processors — a mirror obligation.
Both require that any subcontracted PII processing is governed by the same obligations as the primary processor, ensuring consistent protection throughout the sub-processing chain.

27018 A.11.12 requires equivalent contractual obligations for sub-processors; 27701-8.5.8 additionally requires advance notification of sub-processor changes, making 27701 a superset of the sub-contractor governance obligation.

27018 A.11.13 requires that pre-used data storage is sanitized before re-allocation to prevent access to prior tenant's PII; 27701-6.6.4 (system and application access control) addresses preventing unauthorized access but does not specifically cover storage re-use in multi-tenant cloud environments.
Both require the processor to document and disclose the geographical locations (countries/regions) where PII is or may be stored or processed, enabling the controller to assess transfer risks.
Both require maintaining records of which countries and jurisdictions hold or process the PII, directly mapping the geographic location disclosure requirement to the records obligation.
Both require establishing a legal basis or mechanism for any cross-border transfer of PII, ensuring that international transfers comply with applicable privacy law and contractual obligations.

27018 A.12.2 covers the processor's obligation to identify the legal basis for PII transfer; 27701-7.5.1 covers the same obligation from the controller perspective, making them mirror requirements for different roles in the same transfer chain.

27018 A.3.2 prohibits marketing/advertising use by the processor without PII principal consent; 27701-7.2.3 (from the controller side) requires determining when and how consent must be obtained before processing, including for marketing purposes.

Both require the PII processor to assist in fulfilling obligations to PII principals (data subjects), including responding to subject access requests and other rights, with 27018 framing this as a cooperation obligation and 27701 as a formal assistance obligation.

27018 A.10.1 (breach notification) requires an operational response; 27701-5.6.1 (operational planning and control) requires that operational processes for PII protection — including incident response — are planned and controlled, providing the PIMS management-system context for breach notification.

27018 A.6.1 requires the processor to notify the controller of disclosure requests from law enforcement; 27701-7.3.4 (from the controller side) requires mechanisms for PII principals to exercise rights, but both ultimately support the principal's right to know about disclosures of their data.

27018 A.11.4 (protecting media leaving premises) aligns with 27701-6.8.2 (equipment security) which addresses secure handling of equipment including media outside the organization's physical perimeter.

27018 A.11.3 requires logging of data restoration operations; 27701-6.9.3 (backup) requires that backup and restoration procedures protect PII integrity and confidentiality, providing the management-system control within which restoration logging applies.

27018 A.10.2 requires retention of administrative security policies; 27701-5.5.5 (documented information) requires control of documented information within the PIMS, which subsumes all retention requirements for PIMS documentation.

27018 A.11.2 restricts hardcopy creation of PII; 27701-6.6.3 (user responsibilities) requires users to follow information-handling procedures, which should encompass restrictions on creating unnecessary PII copies.

Unique user ID requirements in 27018 A.11.8 support accountability tracking that feeds into the privacy risk assessment process required by 27701-5.4.1, where the ability to attribute actions to individuals is a risk mitigation measure.

27018 A.12.1 (geographical location of PII) is the processor-side disclosure of where PII is stored; 27701-7.5.2 is the controller-side obligation to document countries and international organizations to which PII can be transferred — both support the same transfer-transparency goal.

27018 A.3.1 (processor purpose limitation) corresponds to 27701-7.4.2 (limit processing from the controller side), both implementing the privacy-by-design principle of processing PII only for specified purposes.

Both require secure handling and deletion of temporary files containing PII — 27018 A.5.1 from the cloud processor perspective and 27701-7.4.6 from the controller perspective — directly parallel controls at different role levels.

27018 A.10.3 (PII return, transfer and disposal at contract end) is the processor obligation; 27701-7.4.8 (disposal) is the controller obligation to ensure secure disposal — they are mirror obligations applying the same disposal requirement to different roles.

Both require security controls for PII transmitted over networks, with 27018 A.11.6 specifying encryption over public data-transmission networks and 27701-6.10.2 providing PIMS-extended guidance on information transfer security.

27018 A.11.7 addresses secure disposal of hardcopy PII materials in cloud operations; 27701-6.9.1 (operational procedures and responsibilities) provides the broader operational management framework within which hardcopy disposal procedures must be defined.

Both require maintaining records of PII disclosures to third parties — 27018 A.6.2 from the processor perspective and 27701-7.5.4 from the controller perspective — providing equivalent accountability at different roles in the processing chain.

27018 A.11.9 requires a current list of authorized users for PII access as part of cloud access governance; 27701-6.6.2 mandates formal user access management processes that encompass maintaining such authorization records.

27018 A.11.13 requires sanitization of pre-used storage before reuse to prevent access to prior PII; 27701-6.5.3 (media handling) covers media disposal and re-use broadly, with 27018 adding the cloud multi-tenancy context.

27018 A.8.1 (disclosure of sub-contracted PII processing) supports the supplier relationship transparency requirement in 27701-6.12.1, which requires that information security and privacy requirements be addressed in supplier agreements.
Both require that processing arrangements with service providers/sub-processors are governed by formal contracts that include appropriate privacy and security requirements.

27018 A.10.1 specifies breach notification to the customer for PII-related incidents in cloud environments; 27701-6.13.1 provides the full PIMS incident management framework including reporting obligations that encompasses 27018's notification requirement.
